title: pouteria microphylla
intro: Pouteria microphylla, also known as the Amazonian lapacho or Taheebo, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pouteria microphylla include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Pouteria microphylla are typically made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and are also used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
